l1: Is this the only machine that is showing this behavior, if not how many?
How do they recover when its happening? (Wait? Reboot? Hard shutdown? Kill a program?)
How do they know the issue is happening? (What symptoms they see, end users? Admins?)
What is the behaviour when issue is occurring?
Is it always the same symptoms? What is the same, what is different?
Is the issue gradual or sudden?
Does the issue happen randomly?
Is there any associated events being logged?
Error message. Typically applications that hang do not generate errors. Noting any pop-up is very helpful?
What are the steps to repro if applicable? Need to be as precise as we can in this documentation as it usually helps significantly when reviewing data.
l2: Is WMI running locally? Is WMI running remotely?
Did you try connecting through Wbemtest?
Check the status of the winmgmt service?
What is the size of the repository?
If it is a remote issue can you ping the server when the issue occurs?
Are you able to telnet the remote server over port 135?
If there are DCOM errors on the event viewer, how frequently does the DCOM error occur? Which DCOM error?
Is the issue with only a single query or command or all WMI commands?

l1: General information about the Server or Workstation
Role of the machine if Server (eg. file server, DC, application server)
Virtualization (e.g. VMware or Hyper-V) or Physical machine
Ask the Customer to describe the issue and who is impacted?
What is the name of the application or process that is having the issue?
When did the problem start?
How often has it occurred? Specific days and times it occurs?
When did the problem occur last?
Can we repro on demand and quickly?
l2: Are we getting any error?
Does the issue occur on machines in the same subnet or just on a LAN or WAN?
When did the issue start occurring? What has changed?
How did you get notified about the problem? Did the end-users call the helpdesk?

l1: Check the status of WMI service
Check the local WMI functionality (WMIC, WBEMTEST and WMIMGMT.msc)
Try connecting to the remote machine from any two of the following: (WMIC, WBEMTEST and WMIMGMT.msc)
Check the status of WMI service

Verify WMI Namespace Permissions. In wmimgmt.msc, Right click on WMI Control(local) and select Properties. Go to the Security tab -> Expand the Root folder and select the CIMV2 namespace -> Click on Security and add the user account -> Enable the following permissions Enable Account, Remote Enable.

Check DCOM Permissions. Open dcomcnfg -> Right click My Computer -> Properties -> Under COM security, click Edit Limits for both sections -> Give the user Remote Launch and Remote Activation permissions -> Find Windows Management Instrumentation in DCOM config and give the user Remote Launch and Remote Activation permissions
Run the winmgmt /verifyrepository
l2: Check Group Policy Settings. Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> System -> Remote Procedure Call. Set (Restrictions for Unauthenticated RPC clients) and (RPC Endpoint Mapper Client Authentication) to Disabled or Not Configured
Check Registry Settings. Ensure the registry value restrict anonymous is not set to 2. It is located under HKLM/System/CurrentControlSet/Control/Lsa
Set the following registry values -> HKLM/SOFTWARE/Microsoft/WBEM/CIMOM/AllowAnonymousCallback=1 and HKLM/SOFTWARE/Microsoft/Windows/CurrentVersion/Policies/System/LocalAccountTokenFilterPolicy=1
Disable UAC Remote Restriction. HKLM/SOFTWARE/Microsoft/Windows/CurrentVersion/Policies/System/LocalAccountTokenFilterPolicy=1. Restart the WinRM service.
Check Firewall Settings. Ensure the Windows Firewall is not blocking the connections. Allow the Remote Administration rule in Windows Firewall.

Verify Domain Membership. Ensure both machines are in the same domain or subnet. If Machine A is a domain member, ensure the local user account on Machine B has the same username and password as the domain user account on Machine A

l1: Check the event logs while replicating the issue (system logs and the WMI activity logs)
Collect gpresult
Collect system information using systeminfo command
l2: Collect procmon at the time of reproducing the issue
Collect TSS for WMI
Capture a network trace
